summary.intent: Address three P2 Codex review findings on PR #710 (ars-ui GridList component): item-only grid indexing for sectioned collections, stale non-composite cells out of tab order, and convenience typeahead timestamps using a real clock.
summary.outcome: All three findings were fixed in a follow-up commit, CI passed fully, Codex review threads were resolved, and the changes were pushed to the PR branch with tests and coverage intact.

repo: GridList lives in crates/ars-components/src/data_display/grid_list/mod.rs and has a tight branch coverage threshold that must be maintained.
The project uses cargo xtask ci clippy for linting, cargo +nightly fmt for formatting, cargo insta test for snapshot/spec conformance, and cargo xcov for coverage.
Timestamp helpers in selection components follow a pattern: _at takes Duration, and a convenience helper calls current_time() with a last-key-plus-1ms fallback when no clock is available.
Codex review is triggered via @codex review comment on the GitHub issue; review status is tracked via the eyes reaction and unresolved GraphQL threads.
The repo uses a snapshot-reviewed label that must be reapplied after any commit that updates insta snapshots.
Structural collection nodes (sections, headers, separators) must not consume grid slots; navigation/range/position helpers must iterate real item nodes only.
Non-composite listitem rows must omit aria-selected and expose selection state only through data-ars-selected.

code.finding: Cell identity and stale-key inertness logic; stale non-composite keys should be disabled/inert (tabindex=-1) with no aria-selected.
Initialization path was using flat collection length; needs to use real item count only to avoid counting structural nodes.
Navigation target, range_keys, grid_dimensions, and cell_position helpers; these scan loops initially used match but Clippy requires if let for single-arm patterns.

workflow: Clippy single_match lint fires on scan loops that use match with only one non-trivial arm; convert to if let.
When a patch context doesn't match exactly, split into smaller patches targeting exact current lines rather than retrying the large patch.
Codex P2 findings should be assessed for technical validity before implementing; all three here were valid and addressed.
Poll CI with exponentially increasing sleep intervals (30s → 60s → 90s → 120s) as long-tail jobs like coverage and release verification run much longer than formatting/clippy.
After pushing a review-fix commit, resolve GitHub review threads via GraphQL mutation and post per-thread reply comments citing the fix commit SHA.

friction: First large patch failed because the nearby code context differed slightly from expected; required splitting into multiple smaller targeted patches.
CI long-tail jobs (coverage, release verification, Dioxus feature flags) required many polling cycles totaling ~10+ minutes of waiting.
GitHub checks API did not always reflect the latest state immediately, causing several no-op polling rounds.
Clippy caught two single_match patterns in newly written scan loops that required an additional fix-and-recheck cycle.
open_items: Coverage is at exactly the branch threshold for ars-components — any future changes that add uncovered branches will fail CI.
The convenience typeahead timestamp helper's clock fallback (last-key-plus-1ms) is a workaround for environments without std; this debt is shared with the select component.
